hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming favorable interactions with SSTR2-unique ECL2 residues (e.g., E192, E193), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target binding by avoiding clashes with SSTR3/SSTR5 pocket residues.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to achieve SSTR2 selectivity. We must explicitly target and disrupt off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the native pharmacophore and disulfide bridge.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on them will allow us to introduce mutations that disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving pharmacophore positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without compromising the pharmacophore core (FWKT at positions 7-10) or disulfide bridge (Cys3/Cys14).
